sections.paragraph:
νόμισμα, ατος, τό (νομίζω; primary mng. ‘anything sanctioned by common usage’: Aeschyl. et al.) money officially introduced into common use, coin (so Hdt., Aristoph. et al.; ins; PTebt 485 [II b.c.]; PGrenf II, 77, 8; 2 Esdr 17: 72 v.l.; 1 Macc 15:6; EpArist 33; Philo, Spec. Leg. 2, 33; Jos., Bell. 2, 592, Ant. 14, 371) τὸ ν. τοῦ κήνσου the coin for paying the tax Mt 22:19 (foll. by Just., A I, 17, 2 τίνος εἰκόνα τὸ ν. ἔχει;). Pl. (Herodian 1, 9, 7), as metaph. (Philo is also fond of such usage) νομίσματα δύο, ὸ̔ μὲν θεοῦ, ὸ̔ δὲ κόσμου two coinages, one of God and the other of the world of the believers and the unbelievers IMg 5:2.—B. 775. DELG s.v. νέμω Ic. M-M.

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

PGrenf II PGrenf II = BGrenfell/AHunt, New Classical Fragments—Lists 4

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

foll. foll. = followed, following

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Herodian Herodian = Herodianus Historicus, III a.d.—List 5

metaph. metaph. = metaphor(ically)

IMg IMg = Ignatius to the Magnesians—List 1

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 676
